data.narrative:
Reliable Robotics is a Series C-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised $134M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of five private Series C companies in the same sector (Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, and Isar Aerospace) and six public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $134M raised as a proxy base value and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $228M. Peer-median anchoring across the five same-sector, same-stage private companies yields a $1.5B implied valuation, with the peer set ranging from $1.0B (Isar Aerospace) to $2.5B (Axiom Space). Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of revenue disclosure for Reliable Robotics. The sector median P/S for public Space & Aerospace comparables is 64.2x across six tickers.

The triangulated valuation range is $194M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $864M. This wide range reflects tension between the stage-progression method, which anchors to capital deployed, and the peer-median method, which captures current market pricing for comparable private companies. Key uncertainties include capital intensity relative to peers, time-to-revenue, and whether Reliable Robotics' specific applications command premium or discount valuations within the sector.
